response:
Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day. To find out how many bottles she needs for a leap year, we first calculate the total number of days in a leap year, which is 366 days. Since she uses shampoo every other day, she will use it 366 / 2 = 183 times. Each bottle contains 14 ounces of shampoo, so the total amount of shampoo needed is 183 * 1/4 = 45.75 ounces. This means she needs 46 ounces of shampoo to last through the year. Since each bottle contains 14 ounces, she will need [46 / 14] = [3.2857] bottles. However, she can't buy a fraction of a bottle, so she will need [4] bottles to have enough shampoo.

Brittany will need [4] bottles of shampoo to get through a leap year. Each bottle contains 14 ounces, so she will use 14 * 4 = 56 ounces of shampoo. This is enough for 183 uses, which is 366 days, every other day. She will have [56 - 45.75] = [10.25] ounces of shampoo left over. This ensures she has enough shampoo for the entire year.
